title: symphyotrichum × subgeminatum
intro: Symphyotrichum × subgeminatum, also known as 'Bandana', is a compact, low-growing perennial plant that is a hybrid of the Symphyotrichum novae-angliae and Symphyotrichum novi-belgii species. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Symphyotrichum × subgeminatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. It can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
